title: The Seventh Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## July 24, 2022 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Seventh Sunday after Pentecost, falling within Ordinary Time in the church calendar. The Revised Common Lectionary for this date typically includes 2 Kings 4:42-44 (Elisha feeding the people), Psalm 145:10-18, Ephesians 3:14-21, and John 6:1-21 (the feeding of the five thousand and Jesus walking on water)—texts centered on divine provision and abundance. The season emphasizes growth in faith and discipleship during the extended period after Easter.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of its 80th General Convention, held in Baltimore (July 7-14, 2022), which had just concluded ten days before this sermon. The convention addressed significant issues including LGBTQ+ inclusion in church leadership and revisions to marriage liturgies in the Book of Common Prayer. The Diocese of Colorado would have been processing these national decisions and their implications for local parishes.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in the midst of an exceptionally hot and dry summer in 2022, with record-breaking temperatures and severe drought conditions affecting Colorado throughout July. The week before this sermon saw Denver experiencing temperatures in the high 90s with ongoing wildfire threats across the state. Politically, Colorado was in mid-campaign season with primary elections held on June 28, and candidates for governor (Democrat Jared Polis, Republican Heidi Ganahl, and others) were actively campaigning.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The week of July 18-24 was dominated by the January 6 House Committee televised hearings, which had captivated national attention with former Trump officials and allies testifying about the Capitol insurrection. The Highland Park, Illinois mass shooting (July 4) remained in the news cycle with ongoing coverage of the shooting's investigation and gun control debates. The stock market continued volatile trading amid inflation concerns and Federal Reserve rate-hiking expectations, with the S&P 500 having entered bear market territory in June.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine remained the dominant international story, with Russian forces continuing their assault and advancing in the Donbas region despite Ukrainian resistance and Western military aid. The humanitarian crisis worsened with grain shipments blocked and global food security threatened, directly affecting developing nations. Monkeypox cases were surging globally with the WHO preparing to declare a public health emergency (declared August 4, 2022).

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American society in late July 2022 was deeply polarized and anxious about democratic institutions, inflation's impact on daily life, and future stability. Culturally, summer entertainment was in full swing with Marvel's "Thor: Love and Thunder" dominating box offices, and the nation was in the grip of a heat wave affecting much of the country. The zeitgeist reflected exhaustion from ongoing political divisions, economic uncertainty, and a sense of living through compounding crises—creating a moment when sermons about divine provision and faith amid uncertainty would have carried particular weight.
